Home / Alternatives to Zapier / Zapier vs Activepieces

Zapier vs Activepieces

A side-by-side look at Zapier (the paid SaaS) and Activepieces (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Zapier Activepieces
Tagline Connect thousands of apps with no-code automations. Open source Zapier alternative with MIT license.
License Proprietary SaaS MIT
Pricing Free tier (100 tasks/month); Starter from $19.99/month. Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 2/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Web only
Mobile apps Official apps typically available None official
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

If you want a strictly MIT-licensed, truly-open alternative.

Activepieces strengths

  • Properly MIT-licensed and open.
  • Visual flows with branching and loops.
  • TypeScript-based custom pieces.

Activepieces weaknesses

  • Smaller integration catalogue than Zapier or n8n.
  • Newer project — breaking changes occur.
  • Cloud pricing tiers still evolving.

What's the catch with Zapier?

  • Pricing scales with task volume quickly.
  • Data flows through Zapier cloud.
  • Self-hosting not possible.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Zapier: see Zapier alternatives, or learn more about Activepieces on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
